subject: physics
summary_en: Magnetic field $\vec{B}$ exerts force on moving charges: $\vec{F} = q\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$. On current-carrying wires: $\vec{F} = I\vec{L} \times \vec{B}$. Magnetic forces do NO work — they only change direction of motion.
summary_he: שדה מגנטי $\vec{B}$ מפעיל כוח על מטענים נעים: $\vec{F} = q\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$. על תיל זרם: $\vec{F} = I\vec{L} \times \vec{B}$. כוחות מגנטיים **לא** עושים עבודה.

sections.body_en_md:
Magnets stick to fridges. Compass needles point north. Speakers vibrate. All of this is **magnetism**.

Unlike gravity or the electric field, magnetism affects only MOVING charges (and the current-carrying wires they form).

### Properties of $\vec{B}$ field

- Symbol: $\vec{B}$. Unit: **tesla** (T). $1 \, \text{T} = 1 \, \text{kg}/(\text{A} \cdot \text{s}^2)$.
- Earth's field: $\sim 50 \, \mu\text{T}$ at surface.
- Strong magnet (MRI): $\sim 1.5$ T.
- Neutron star: $10^{10}$ T.

### Sources

- Moving charges (currents in wires).
- Spinning electrons in atoms (basis of ferromagnetism in iron, nickel).

### Field lines

- Closed loops (unlike electric field lines which start/end on charges).
- From N-pole to S-pole OUTSIDE the magnet; S to N INSIDE.
- **No magnetic monopoles** — every magnet has both N and S.
sections.body_he_md:
מגנטים נדבקים למקרר. מצפן מצביע לצפון. רמקולים רוטטים. הכל **מגנטיות**.

שונה מכבידה/חשמלי: משפיע **רק על מטענים נעים** (וזרמים).

### תכונות $\vec{B}$

- יחידה: **טסלה** (T).
- שדה כדור הארץ: $\sim 50\,\mu$T.
- MRI: $\sim 1.5$ T.
- כוכב נויטרונים: $10^{10}$ T.

### מקורות

- מטענים נעים (זרמים).
- ספין אלקטרונים (פרומגנטיות).

### קווי שדה

- לולאות סגורות.
- מ-N ל-S בחוץ.
- **אין מונופולים** — כל מגנט יש לו N ו-S.

sections.title_en: Lorentz force, wire force, motion in fields
sections.title_he: כוח לורנץ, כוח על תיל, תנועה בשדות
sections.body_en_md:
### Lorentz force (single charge)

$$\boxed{\vec{F} = q \vec{v} \times \vec{B}.}$$

Magnitude: $F = |q| v B \sin\theta$ (where $\theta$ = angle between $\vec{v}$ and $\vec{B}$).

Direction: by RIGHT-HAND RULE.
- Point fingers in direction of $\vec{v}$.
- Curl them toward $\vec{B}$.
- Thumb gives direction of $\vec{F}$ for POSITIVE charge.
- For negative: opposite.

**Key:** If $\vec{v} \parallel \vec{B}$ (parallel), no force. Force is ALWAYS perpendicular to $\vec{v}$.

### No work

$\vec{F} \perp \vec{v} \Rightarrow \vec{F} \cdot \vec{v} = 0$. Magnetic force does NO WORK; speed (and kinetic energy) is unchanged. Only direction changes.

### Circular motion of charge in uniform $B$

If $\vec{v} \perp \vec{B}$: charge moves in a CIRCLE. Magnetic force = centripetal:

$$qvB = \frac{mv^2}{r} \Rightarrow \boxed{r = \frac{mv}{|q|B}.}$$

Frequency of circular motion (cyclotron frequency):

$$f_c = \frac{|q|B}{2\pi m}.$$

Used in mass spectrometers and particle accelerators.

### Force on current-carrying wire

Wire of length $L$ carrying current $I$ in field $\vec{B}$:

$$\boxed{\vec{F} = I \vec{L} \times \vec{B}, \quad F = I L B \sin\theta.}$$

Direction: right-hand rule (using $\vec{L}$ in direction of $I$).

This is how electric motors work.

### Field of a long straight wire

At distance $d$ from an infinite straight wire carrying current $I$:

$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2 \pi d},$$

where $\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7} \, \text{T} \cdot \text{m/A}$ (permeability of free space).

Field CIRCLES around the wire (right-hand rule: thumb in $I$, fingers curl in $\vec{B}$).

### Solenoid field

Inside a long solenoid (many turns): $B = \mu_0 n I$, where $n$ = turns per unit length. Field is roughly uniform inside.
sections.body_he_md:
### כוח לורנץ

$$\boxed{\vec{F} = q\vec{v} \times \vec{B}.}$$

גודל: $F = |q|vB\sin\theta$. כיוון: יד ימין.

**מפתח:** $\vec{v} \parallel \vec{B}$ → אין כוח. $\vec{F}$ תמיד ניצב ל-$\vec{v}$.

### אין עבודה

$\vec{F} \perp \vec{v} \Rightarrow$ אין עבודה. אנרגיה קינטית נשמרת.

### תנועה מעגלית

אם $\vec{v} \perp \vec{B}$:

$$r = mv/(|q|B).$$

תדירות ציקלוטרון: $f_c = |q|B/(2\pi m)$.

### כוח על תיל זרם

$$\boxed{F = ILB\sin\theta.}$$

בסיס למנועים חשמליים.

### שדה תיל ארוך

$$B = \mu_0 I / (2\pi d).$$

$\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}$ T·m/A. שדה סובב סביב התיל.

### שדה סולנואיד

$B = \mu_0 n I$ בפנים.

sections.body_en_md:
An electron moves at $v = 2 \times 10^6$ m/s perpendicular to a 0.01 T magnetic field. Find (a) the magnetic force magnitude, (b) the radius of its circular path, (c) cyclotron frequency.

**(a) Force:** $F = |q|vB = (1.6 \times 10^{-19})(2 \times 10^6)(0.01) = 3.2 \times 10^{-15}$ N.

**(b) Radius:** $r = mv/(|q|B) = (9.11 \times 10^{-31})(2 \times 10^6)/[(1.6 \times 10^{-19})(0.01)]$.

$$r = (1.822 \times 10^{-24})/(1.6 \times 10^{-21}) \approx 1.14 \times 10^{-3} \, \text{m} = 1.14 \, \text{mm}.$$

**(c) Frequency:** $f_c = |q|B/(2\pi m) = (1.6 \times 10^{-19})(0.01)/[2\pi (9.11 \times 10^{-31})]$.

$$f_c = (1.6 \times 10^{-21})/(5.72 \times 10^{-30}) \approx 2.80 \times 10^8 \, \text{Hz} = 280 \, \text{MHz}.$$

**Answer:** $F \approx 3.2 \times 10^{-15}$ N, $r \approx 1.14$ mm, $f_c \approx 280$ MHz.

Notice: an electron in 0.01 T spirals at ~280 MHz — the basis of microwave electronics.
sections.body_he_md:
אלקטרון ב-$v = 2\times10^6$ מ"ש ניצב לשדה $B = 0.01$ T. (א) כוח, (ב) רדיוס, (ג) תדירות?

**(א)** $F = (1.6\times10^{-19})(2\times10^6)(0.01) = 3.2\times10^{-15}$ N.
**(ב)** $r = mv/(|q|B) \approx 1.14$ מ"מ.
**(ג)** $f_c \approx 280$ MHz.

sections.body_en_md:
1. **'Magnetic force can do work.'** NO. $\vec{F} \perp \vec{v}$, so $\vec{F} \cdot \vec{v} = 0$.
2. **Direction of force.** Right-hand rule for $\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$. For negative charge: flip.
3. **Stationary charge in field.** No force ($v = 0$).
4. **$\vec{v} \parallel \vec{B}$.** No force ($\sin 0 = 0$).
5. **Field lines: start/end?** They don't (closed loops; no monopoles).
6. **Confusing $\vec{B}$ with $\vec{E}$.** They have different units (T vs. N/C) and different force laws.
7. **Forgetting $\sin\theta$.** $F = qvB\sin\theta$. Only perpendicular component contributes.
sections.body_he_md:
1. **'כוח מגנטי עושה עבודה'** — לא.
2. **כיוון כוח** — יד ימין; שלילי הפוך.
3. **מטען נח** — אין כוח.
4. **$\vec{v}\parallel\vec{B}$** — אין כוח.
5. **קווי שדה לא מתחילים/מסתיימים.**
6. **$\vec{B}$ מול $\vec{E}$.**
7. **לא לשכוח $\sin\theta$.**

sections.body_en_md:
- $\vec{F} = q\vec{v} \times \vec{B}$, $F = |q|vB\sin\theta$.
- Direction by right-hand rule.
- Magnetic force does NO work; only deflects.
- Circular motion: $r = mv/(|q|B)$.
- Wire force: $\vec{F} = I\vec{L}\times\vec{B}$.
- Wire field: $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$.
- Solenoid: $B = \mu_0 n I$.
- No magnetic monopoles.
sections.body_he_md:
- $\vec{F} = q\vec{v}\times\vec{B}$.
- יד ימין.
- אין עבודה.
- $r = mv/(|q|B)$.
- תיל: $F = ILB\sin\theta$.
- שדה תיל: $\mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$.
- סולנואיד: $\mu_0 nI$.

questions.stem_en: A stationary positive charge sits in a uniform $\vec{B}$ field. The magnetic force on it is:
questions.stem_he: מטען חיובי **נח** בשדה $\vec{B}$ אחיד. הכוח המגנטי עליו:

questions.stem_en: Magnetic force on a moving charge:
A proton moves at $10^6$ m/s perpendicular to a 0.2 T field. Find the magnetic force magnitude.
questions.stem_he: פרוטון נע ב-$10^6$ מ"ש ניצב לשדה 0.2 T. גודל כוח?
questions.rubric_en:
Full credit (2/2): $F = qvB = (1.6\times10^{-19})(10^6)(0.2) = 3.2\times10^{-14}$ N.
Partial (1/2): correct formula.

questions.stem_en: A charged particle enters a uniform $\vec{B}$ field perpendicular to $\vec{v}$. Its trajectory is:
questions.stem_he: חלקיק טעון נכנס לשדה $\vec{B}$ אחיד ניצב ל-$\vec{v}$. המסלול:
questions.explanation_en: $\vec{F}$ always perpendicular to $\vec{v}$ → uniform circular motion.

questions.stem_en: A wire 0.5 m long carries 3 A current perpendicular to a 0.4 T magnetic field. Find the force on the wire.
questions.stem_he: תיל 0.5 מ' נושא זרם 3 A ניצב לשדה 0.4 T. מצאו כוח.
questions.rubric_en:
Full credit (2/2): $F = ILB\sin 90° = (3)(0.5)(0.4)(1) = 0.6$ N.
Partial (1/2): correct formula.

questions.stem_en: An electron and a proton both move at the same speed perpendicular to the same $\vec{B}$ field. Their orbital radii compare as:
questions.stem_he: אלקטרון ופרוטון נעים באותה מהירות ניצבים לאותו $\vec{B}$. רדיוסים:
questions.explanation_en: $r = mv/|q|B$. Same $v, B, |q|$, so $r \propto m$. Proton's mass is ~1836× electron's → proton orbit is ~1836× larger.

key_insights: Magnetic field affects only MOVING charges.
Right-hand rule for direction (negative charge: flip).
Magnetic force does NO WORK; only changes direction.

common_misconceptions.wrong: Magnetic force can speed up a charge
common_misconceptions.correction: Magnetic force is always perpendicular to $\vec{v}$ → no work → no speed change. Only direction changes.

agent_hints.tutor_pacing_hint: Stress: $\vec{B}$ affects ONLY moving charges. Right-hand rule needs practice — demo with hands. Magnetic force does no work; this is non-intuitive but crucial.

diagnostic_signals.if_learner_thinks_b_does_work: $\vec{F} \perp \vec{v}$ always. Imagine pushing perpendicular to motion — never speeds it up.
diagnostic_signals.if_learner_uses_left_hand: Right-hand rule (for positive charge). For negative, flip.
